data.narrative:
Orbit Fab is a Series A-stage space infrastructure company that has raised $28.5M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 5 public-market comps in Space & Aerospace (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage peers including ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Stoke Space, Aalyria, and Sift, with stage distribution spanning Series A through Series B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to $28.5M in total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A progression to the next funding stage, implies a valuation of $57M. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range of $274M (Sift, Series B) to $1.3B (Aalyria, Series B). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S multiple is 45.3x across 6 public tickers, Orbit Fab's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated range across applicable methods is $48M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $228M. This wide band reflects the stage and maturity dispersion within the peer set, particularly the premium valuations of Series B competitors. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, near-term funding trajectory, and whether Orbit Fab's specific orbital servicing focus commands a valuation premium or discount to broader aerospace comparables.
